 {"id":"bd-15yvf","title":"Task: Implement context pack caching layer","description":"# Task: Context Pack Caching

Cache generated context packs for reuse across ensemble runs.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/context_cache.go
type ContextCache struct {
    CacheDir string
    TTL      time.Duration
}

type CachedPack struct {
    Pack        *ContextPack
    Hash        string
    CachedAt    time.Time
    ExpiresAt   time.Time
    HitCount    int
}

func NewContextCache(cacheDir string, ttl time.Duration) *ContextCache
func (c *ContextCache) Get(projectRoot string) (*ContextPack, bool)
func (c *ContextCache) Put(projectRoot string, pack *ContextPack) error
func (c *ContextCache) Invalidate(projectRoot string) error
func (c *ContextCache) InvalidateAll() error
func (c *ContextCache) ComputeHash(pack *ContextPack) string
func (c *ContextCache) IsStale(pack *CachedPack) bool
```

## Cache Key
```
key = sha256(project_root + git_head_commit + problem_statement)
```

## Invalidation Triggers
- Git HEAD changes (new commits)
- Problem statement changes
- User explicit invalidation (--no-cache)
- TTL expiration (default 1 hour)

## Cache Location
```
~/.cache/ntm/context-packs/
├── <hash1>.json
├── <hash2>.json
└── metadata.json
```

## Logging
- Log cache hits/misses
- Log invalidation reasons
- Track hit rate in metrics

 {"id":"bd-16291","title":"Add ntm controller command to launch a dedicated controller agent in pane 1 with preset prompt","description":"Add ntm controller command to launch a dedicated controller agent in pane 1 with preset prompt.

## Problem Being Solved

Multi-agent swarms benefit from having a "controller" agent that coordinates the others. Currently this requires manual setup.

## Command Design

```bash
ntm controller SESSION [--prompt FILE] [--agent-type cc|cod|gmi]
```

### Behavior
1. Attaches to SESSION
2. Finds or creates pane 1
3. Launches agent with controller-specific prompt
4. Default prompt includes session overview and coordination instructions

### Default Controller Prompt
```
You are the controller agent for session {SESSION}.
Other agents in this session: {AGENT_LIST}
Your role: coordinate work, prevent conflicts, ensure quality.
Use ntm robot commands to monitor other agents.
```

### Custom Prompt
User can provide custom prompt file with variables:
- {SESSION}: session name
- {AGENT_LIST}: list of other agents
- {PROJECT}: project directory
- {BEADS}: open beads (if bv available)

## Robot Mode API
ntm --robot-controller-spawn=SESSION [--prompt=FILE]
Returns: JSON with controller pane info

## Acceptance Criteria

1. ntm controller command exists and shows in help
2. Command spawns agent in pane 1 of specified session
3. Default prompt includes session name and agent list
4. --prompt flag accepts path to custom prompt file
5. --agent-type flag allows choosing agent type (default: cc)
6. Robot mode variant returns JSON with pane ID

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/cmd/controller_test.go
- Test prompt variable substitution
- Test pane selection logic

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-CONTROLLER] Controller Command Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-CONTROLLER]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing ntm controller command"
TEST_SESSION="e2e_ctrl_$$"
ntm spawn "$TEST_SESSION" --cc=2 --no-prompt
sleep 5
# Test controller spawn
ntm controller "$TEST_SESSION" --no-prompt 2>&1 || echo "WARN: controller command not yet implemented"
ntm kill "$TEST_SESSION" --force
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Controller test complete"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. ntm controller command launches dedicated agent in pane 1
2. Preset prompt includes project context
3. Agent has elevated permissions for coordination
4. Integration with agent mail for swarm control
5. Unit tests for command parsing
6. E2E test for controller lifecycle","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"CloudyGlen","created_at":"2026-01-20T05:27:38.891522309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:57:17.132978631Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-168bh","title":"Unit Tests: Watch mode loop and exit conditions","description":"## Test Coverage
Unit tests for watch mode loop and exit conditions.

## Test Cases

### Watch Loop
- Initial assignment pass executes
- Completion detected triggers reassignment
- Unblocked beads detected and assigned
- Poll interval respected

### Exit Conditions
- Ctrl+C (SIGINT) triggers graceful shutdown
- --stop-when-done exits when no beads
- All agents crashed exits with error
- No more idle agents waits

### Streaming Output
- Timestamps accurate
- Assignment events logged
- Completion events logged
- Unblock events logged

### Error Handling
- BV unavailable mid-loop
- Agent Mail unavailable
- Assignment failure recovery

### Edge Cases
- Zero beads to start
- All beads blocked
- Single agent scenario
- Very fast completions

## Logging
- Log each loop iteration
- Log event processing
- Log exit reason

## Test File

 {"id":"bd-1s6cs","title":"TOON: Add --robot-output-format flag to all robot commands","description":"Add output format flag to the robot command system.

## Approach

Robot commands share a common infrastructure. Add format flag at the top level so all commands inherit it.

## Flag Definition

```go
var robotOutputFormat string

func init() {
    rootCmd.PersistentFlags().StringVar(&robotOutputFormat, 
        "robot-output-format", 
        "json",
        "Output format for robot commands (json, toon)")
}
```

## Integration with Serializer

```go
func getRobotSerializer() serialize.Serializer {
    format := serialize.Format(robotOutputFormat)
    if format == "" {
        format = serialize.Format(config.Robot.Output.Format)
    }
    return serialize.NewSerializer(format)
}
```

## Update Robot Commands

All robot commands that output data need to use the serializer.

## Code Location
- internal/robot/flags.go (new)
- Update all files in internal/robot/

## Acceptance Criteria

1. --robot-output-format flag registered as PersistentFlag in root.go
2. Flag accepts "json" and "toon" values
3. Invalid format value returns clear error message
4. Flag overrides config file setting when specified
5. All robot commands use the serializer for output
6. Default value is "json" for backwards compatibility

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/robot/flags_test.go
- Test flag parsing, override behavior, invalid values

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# [E2E-TOON-FLAG] Robot Output Format Flag Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-TOON-FLAG]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing --robot-output-format flag"
# Test JSON output (default)
ntm --robot-list-sessions 2>/dev/null | jq -e '.' || exit 1
echo "$LOG_PREFIX JSON output valid"
# Test TOON output (if tru installed)
if command -v tru &>/dev/null; then
  ntm --robot-list-sessions --robot-output-format toon 2>/dev/null | grep -q ':' || exit 1
  echo "$LOG_PREFIX TOON output valid"
fi
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. All robot commands accept --robot-output-format flag
2. Values: json (default), toon
3. Flag registered in root.go
4. Default from config [robot.output]
5. Unit tests for flag parsing
6. E2E test verifying TOON output","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:04:03.960119816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T20:55:33.926071926Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1s6cs","depends_on_id":"bd-1zrjo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:25Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1s6cs","depends_on_id":"bd-6wc0a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:25Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"bd-1s6cs","author":"QuietCanyon","text":"Updated E2E snippet to use tru-only (no toon Node CLI).","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:55:33Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1s9b","title":"Task: Implement proficiency config storage","description":"## Overview
Create configuration module for storing and retrieving user proficiency tier.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/config/proficiency.go:
   - ProficiencyConfig struct with Tier, UsageStats, PromotionHistory
   - Load() - read from ~/.config/ntm/proficiency.json
   - Save() - persist to config file
   - GetTier() - return current tier (env override > config > default)

2. Configuration file format (proficiency.json):
   {
     "tier": 1,
     "usage_stats": {
       "commands_run": 150,
       "sessions_created": 25,
       "prompts_sent": 200,
       "first_use": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z",
       "last_use": "2025-01-15T00:00:00Z"
     },
     "promotion_history": [
       {"from": 1, "to": 2, "at": "2025-01-10T00:00:00Z", "reason": "manual"}
     ]
   }

3. Environment override:
   - NTM_PROFICIENCY_TIER=1|2|3 overrides config
   - Useful for scripts that need specific tier behavior

4. Default behavior:
   - New users start at Tier 1
   - Create config file on first use

## Implementation Notes
- Use os.UserConfigDir() for cross-platform config path
- Handle missing/corrupted config gracefully (reset to defaults)
- Log tier changes for debugging

## Acceptance Criteria
- Config persists across sessions
- Env var override works
- Corrupted config recovers gracefully
- Unit tests for load/save cycle","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:24:21.511351381Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:05:42.202497506Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:05:42.202445949Z","close_reason":"Implemented proficiency config in internal/config/proficiency.go with Load/Save/GetTier, env override, usage stats, and unit tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1s9b","depends_on_id":"bd-7ny3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:25Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1s9b","depends_on_id":"bd-p3en","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:25Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1sa5u","title":"process_triage: Add [integrations.process_triage] config section","description":"Add configuration support for process_triage integration.

## Config Schema

```toml
[integrations.process_triage]
# Enable process health monitoring
enabled = true

# Path to pt binary
binary_path = ""

# Check interval in seconds
check_interval = 30

# Thresholds for classification alerts (seconds)
idle_threshold = 300      # 5 minutes
stuck_threshold = 600     # 10 minutes

# Actions on stuck detection
on_stuck = "alert"  # alert, restart, or ignore

# Enable rano correlation for better accuracy
use_rano_data = true
```

## Struct Definition

```go
type ProcessTriageConfig struct {
    Enabled        bool   `toml:"enabled"`
    BinaryPath     string `toml:"binary_path"`
    CheckInterval  int    `toml:"check_interval"`
    IdleThreshold  int    `toml:"idle_threshold"`
    StuckThreshold int    `toml:"stuck_threshold"`
    OnStuck        string `toml:"on_stuck"`
    UseRanoData    bool   `toml:"use_rano_data"`
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:21:46.390650147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T22:43:09.239273765Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T22:43:09.239152136Z","close_reason":"Implemented ProcessTriageConfig with validation and unit tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1sa5u","depends_on_id":"bd-lzvh2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:25Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-33hgw","title":"Task: Implement RedundancyScore metric for output similarity analysis","description":"# Task: RedundancyScore Implementation

Measure similarity between mode outputs to detect unnecessary overlap.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/redundancy.go
type RedundancyScorer struct {
    Outputs []ModeOutput
}

type RedundancyReport struct {
    Overall           float64  // Average pairwise similarity
    PairwiseSimilarity []SimilarityPair
    HighRedundancy    []SimilarityPair // pairs above threshold
    Recommendations   []string
}

type SimilarityPair struct {
    ModeA      string
    ModeB      string
    Similarity float64 // 0-1
    Rationale  string  // why they're similar
}

func NewRedundancyScorer(outputs []ModeOutput) *RedundancyScorer
func (r *RedundancyScorer) Calculate() *RedundancyReport
func (r *RedundancyScorer) Render() string

// Similarity calculation
func (r *RedundancyScorer) calculatePairSimilarity(a, b ModeOutput) float64
func jaccard(setA, setB []string) float64
```

## Similarity Calculation
1. Extract finding texts from both outputs
2. Tokenize and create sets
3. Calculate Jaccard similarity
4. Weight by impact level

## Thresholds
- < 0.3: Low redundancy (good diversity)
- 0.3-0.5: Moderate redundancy (acceptable)
- > 0.5: High redundancy (overlapping insights, suggest replacement)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Calculate pairwise output similarity
- [ ] Generate overall redundancy score
- [ ] Identify high-redundancy pairs
- [ ] Suggest mode replacements for redundant pairs
- [ ] TUI-friendly render method

 {"id":"bd-33tq7","title":"Foundation: Define ProjectAllocation and SwarmPlan types","description":"## Purpose

Define the core data types that represent project allocation decisions and the overall swarm execution plan.

## Implementation

Create `internal/swarm/types.go`:

```go
package swarm

import "time"

// ProjectBeadCount represents a project and its open bead count
type ProjectBeadCount struct {
    Path      string `json:"path"`       // Absolute path to project
    Name      string `json:"name"`       // Project directory name
    OpenBeads int    `json:"open_beads"` // Count of open beads
    Tier      int    `json:"tier"`       // Calculated tier (1, 2, or 3)
}

// ProjectAllocation represents the agent allocation for a single project
type ProjectAllocation struct {
    Project     ProjectBeadCount `json:"project"`
    CCAgents    int              `json:"cc_agents"`
    CodAgents   int              `json:"cod_agents"`
    GmiAgents   int              `json:"gmi_agents"`
    TotalAgents int              `json:"total_agents"`
}

// SwarmPlan is the complete execution plan for the weighted swarm
type SwarmPlan struct {
    // Metadata
    CreatedAt   time.Time `json:"created_at"`
    ScanDir     string    `json:"scan_dir"`
    
    // Per-project allocations
    Allocations []ProjectAllocation `json:"allocations"`
    
    // Aggregate totals
    TotalCC     int `json:"total_cc"`
    TotalCod    int `json:"total_cod"`
    TotalGmi    int `json:"total_gmi"`
    TotalAgents int `json:"total_agents"`
    
    // Session structure
    SessionsPerType int `json:"sessions_per_type"`
    PanesPerSession int `json:"panes_per_session"` // Calculated: ceil(total/sessions)
    
    // Session names to create
    Sessions []SessionSpec `json:"sessions"`
}

// SessionSpec describes a tmux session to create
type SessionSpec struct {
    Name       string     `json:"name"`        // e.g., "cc_agents_1"
    AgentType  string     `json:"agent_type"`  // "cc", "cod", or "gmi"
    PaneCount  int        `json:"pane_count"`
    Panes      []PaneSpec `json:"panes"`
}

// PaneSpec describes a pane within a session
type PaneSpec struct {
    Index       int    `json:"index"`        // 1-based pane index
    Project     string `json:"project"`      // Which project this pane works on
    AgentType   string `json:"agent_type"`
    AgentIndex  int    `json:"agent_index"`  // Agent number within project
    LaunchCmd   string `json:"launch_cmd"`   // "cc", "cod", or "gmi"
}

// SwarmState tracks the runtime state of a running swarm
type SwarmState struct {
    Plan        *SwarmPlan           `json:"plan"`
    StartedAt   time.Time            `json:"started_at"`
    PaneStates  map[string]PaneState `json:"pane_states"` // key: "session:pane"
    LimitHits   []LimitHitEvent      `json:"limit_hits"`
    Respawns    []RespawnEvent       `json:"respawns"`
}

// PaneState tracks individual pane runtime state
type PaneState struct {
    SessionPane   string    `json:"session_pane"`  // "cc_agents_1:1.5"
    AgentType     string    `json:"agent_type"`
    Project       string    `json:"project"`
    Status        string    `json:"status"`        // "running", "limit_hit", "respawning"
    LastActivity  time.Time `json:"last_activity"`
    LimitHitAt    *time.Time `json:"limit_hit_at,omitempty"`
    RespawnCount  int       `json:"respawn_count"`
}

// LimitHitEvent records when an agent hits usage limits
type LimitHitEvent struct {
    SessionPane string    `json:"session_pane"`
    AgentType   string    `json:"agent_type"`
    Project     string    `json:"project"`
    DetectedAt  time.Time `json:"detected_at"`
    Pattern     string    `json:"pattern"`        // Which pattern matched
}

// RespawnEvent records agent respawns
type RespawnEvent struct {
    SessionPane   string    `json:"session_pane"`
    AgentType     string    `json:"agent_type"`
    RespawnedAt   time.Time `json:"respawned_at"`
    AccountRotated bool     `json:"account_rotated"`
    PreviousAccount string  `json:"previous_account,omitempty"`
    NewAccount      string  `json:"new_account,omitempty"`
}
```

## Design Rationale

1. **Separation of Plan vs State**: SwarmPlan is immutable after creation; SwarmState tracks runtime
2. **JSON tags**: Enable robot mode output and state persistence
3. **1-based pane indices**: Match tmux conventions (panes start at 1, not 0)
4. **Hierarchical structure**: Plan → Sessions → Panes makes orchestration straightforward

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All types defined with proper JSON tags
- [ ] Types are well-documented with godoc comments
- [ ] Unit tests for any helper methods
- [ ] Types support JSON marshaling/unmarshaling","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T06:58:26.463622702Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T07:40:10.203282955Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T07:40:10.203192294Z","close_reason":"Implemented all swarm types: ProjectBeadCount, ProjectAllocation, SwarmPlan, SessionSpec, PaneSpec, SwarmState, PaneState, LimitHitEvent, RespawnEvent with full JSON support and comprehensive tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33tq7","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-33tuj","title":"Task: Add output_lines_since_last activity indicator","description":"Add output_lines_since_last field to pane status.

## What

Add `output_lines_since_last` field showing lines of output since last status check.

## Why

Quick activity indicator without timestamp parsing. Useful for:
- Detecting stalled agents (0 lines for extended period)
- Detecting hyperactive agents (excessive output)
- Progress monitoring

## Implementation

Track output line count between status calls:

```go
type OutputTracker struct {
    mu         sync.RWMutex
    lineCount  map[int]int  // pane_index -> total lines seen
}

func (t *OutputTracker) GetDelta(paneIndex int) int {
    t.mu.Lock()
    defer t.mu.Unlock()
    
    current := countPaneLines(paneIndex)
    previous := t.lineCount[paneIndex]
    delta := current - previous
    t.lineCount[paneIndex] = current
    return delta
}
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "panes": [{
    "index": 2,
    "output_lines_since_last": 42
  }]
}
```

## Edge Cases

- First call: return total lines (no previous)
- tmux buffer wraps: handle potential negative delta
- Agent cleared screen: reset tracking","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:36:58.734360851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T01:50:37.002470134Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T01:50:37.002368092Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33tuj","depends_on_id":"bd-1g9wv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"}]}
